rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromide-mediated oxybromination of allylic oximes to form 2-bromomethyl-2-isoxazolines using DMSO as oxygen-free mediator partner
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (Br-) to bromine/tribromide (Br3-), which serves as the active brominating mediator; cathode reduces water to H2 and generates hydroxide that deprotonates the oxime

key_intermediates.name: open-chain bromonium-DMSO adduct B from alkene addition
bromonium intermediate C of allylic oxime

electron_flow: Anode performs 1e- oxidation of Br- to Br•, which dimerizes to Br2 and is sequestered by Br- as Br3-; Br3- brominates DMSO to electrophile A, which transfers Br+ to the alkene of the allylic oxime to give bromonium/sulfonium adduct B (major) or bromonium C (minor); intramolecular oxa-cyclization by the oxime hydroxyl (5-exo-trig) delivers the isoxazoline, while the cathode reduces H2O to H2 and OH- to deprotonate the oxime.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed (0% of 2a) with 3 equiv TEMPO, implying involvement of a radical species in the catalytic cycle

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Bromine-trapped adduct 5 (Ph2C=CHBr / bromo-DPE) detected by GCMS, supporting participation of a bromine radical

notes: Two cyclization pathways are proposed from intermediate A: major pathway goes through DMSO-bromonium adduct B and then ring-closes to 2a; minor pathway invokes a free bromonium C from the oxime which can either close 5-exo-trig (path a → 2a) or 6-endo-trig (path b → by-product 3a, 1,2-oxazine). DMSO acts as Br+ shuttle; in-situ generated hydroxide at the cathode serves as base for oxime deprotonation.
